mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending recession risk, triggering risk-off equity selling and negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in housing starts and consumer credit indicates robust aggregate demand and expanding household balance sheets, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal shows negative predictive power for 60-day forward returns in walk-forward validation over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production relative to steady employment forces firms to pass higher input costs to consumers, expanding profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines coincide with S&P 500 negative returns in > 50% of the instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the 2.5 percent annualized threshold sustains earnings momentum and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: High GDP growth periods yield negative median 5-day S&P 500 returns across all out-of-sample test windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month inflation readings force market participants to reprice higher terminal interest rates, depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI spikes are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in > 55% of instances over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and flat manufacturing output signals a demand-driven economic slowdown, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn adverse.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative 20-day returns in walk-forward validation, showing Rank IC above 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high housing construction activity reflects strong credit availability and consumer confidence, driving short-term equity outperformance and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: High HOUST readings coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in > 60% of observations across all test periods.
